title: SEC0: has_id check misroutes JSON-RPC errors with id:null to notification path
description: File: wasm/src/transport.rs, lines 82-113. The has_id check is: \!value['id'].is_null() && value['id'] \!= Value::Null — both conditions are identical (is_null() tests for Value::Null), so the second condition is redundant. More importantly, JSON-RPC 2.0 allows 'id': null in error responses when the server cannot determine the id of the offending request (spec section 5). If the relay sends {'jsonrpc':'2.0','id':null,'error':{...}}, has_id is false (correct), but then has_method is also false so the frame is silently dropped. However the real danger is the opposite: the relay could send a null-id error whose method field from the notification path is never present. The actual misrouting would occur if a frame has both id:null and a method field — it would be treated as a notification instead of an error response, calling the user's notification callback with a response-shaped frame. The fix is to use value.get('id').is_some() (key present regardless of value) to distinguish responses (id key present, even if null) from notifications (id key absent entirely).
